title: Ealing Studios
content:
{{Top|rwc}}
[[File:Guinness_Kind_Hearts.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Alec Guinness in Ealing comedy [[Wikipedia:Kind Hearts and Coronets|''Kind Hearts and Coronets'']] (1949)]]
'''Ealing Studios''', a [[Wikipedia:television|television]] and [[Wikipedia:film|film]] production company and facilities provider at [[Wikipedia:Ealing|Ealing]] Green in [[Wikipedia:West London|West London]], claims to be the oldest film studio in the world.<ref name=":1">[https://ealingstudios.com/about-ealing/history-3/ Ealing Studios history page] at [https://ealingstudios.com/ EalingStudios.com]</ref> It was used for pick up shots in [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Attack of the Clones'']] in the last week of [[March]] and first week of [[April]] [[2001]] and again in [[October]] to [[November]] the same year.

Scenes shot over twelve days at Ealing in March and April included the [[Geonosis droid factories|droid factory]] sequence; [[Anakin Skywalker]] and [[Padmé Amidala]] in the [[Naboo]] starship; pickups from the [[First Battle of Geonosis|Geonosis arena battle]]; remaining scenes of the fight between [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Jango Fett]]; Anakin's approach to the [[Unidentified Tusken village|Tusken Raider]] camp; [[Theed Royal Palace|Theed Palace]] throne room shots; a ledge scene between Jango and [[Zam Wesell]]; a new scene in [[Palpatine's office]]; and a new [[Jedi Council]] scene.<ref name=":0">[[Mythmaking: Behind the Scenes of Attack of the Clones|Star Wars: Mythmaking: Behind the Scenes of Attack of the Clones]]</ref>

Additional pickups featuring minimal set pieces were shot over six days, wrapping on [[November]] 6, 2001. Brief shots were captured for the final battle with [[Count Dooku]]; [[Shmi Skywalker Lars|Shmi Skywalker]]'s death scene; the Jedi [[First Battle of Geonosis|battle on Geonosis]]; the homestead dinner on [[Tatooine]]; scenes with [[Mace Windu]], Obi-Wan, and [[Yoda]] walking the [[Jedi Temple]] corridors; a new scene between Anakin and [[Palpatine]] in the [[Emperor's office|Chancellor's office]]: and a new elevator scene with Obi-Wan and Anakin.<ref name=":0" />

Ealing Studios produced a series of popular dark comedies starring [[Alec Guinness]] in the late 1940s and early 1950s, including [[Wikipedia:Kind Hearts and Coronets|''Kind Hearts and Coronets'']] (1949), [[Wikipedia:The Man in the White Suit|''The Man in the White Suit'']] (1951), [[Wikipedia:The Lavender Hill Mob|''The Lavender Hill Mob'']] (1952), and [[Wikipedia:The Ladykillers|''The Ladykillers'']] (1955).<ref name=":1" />
